matches.snippet: a flip flap in all Finnyland. Witchman, watch of your night.

matches.matched_text: Like a Finn at a fair
matches.snippet: for who is to wear the lily of Bohemey, Florestan, Thaddeus, Hardress or Myles. And lead raptivity captive. Ready! Like a Finn at a fair.
matches.reasoning: Explicit 'Finn' in common phrase 'Like a Finn at a fair' - Finn MacCool figure invoked colloquially

matches.snippet: Only the caul knows his thousandfirst name. Hocus Crocus, Esquilocus, Finnfinn the Faineant, how feel full foes in furrinarr!
matches.reasoning: Doubled 'Finnfinn' epithet with 'Faineant' (the idle/lazy one) - avatar name for HCE/Finn, possibly alluding to Finn MacCool's sloth or hibernation
